message:
fix(core): replace fallible try_into().unwrap() byte conversions with safe copy_from_slice

Three modules used `bytes[0..8].try_into().unwrap()` to convert hash
output slices into [u8; 8] for u64::from_le_bytes. While the slice
lengths are statically correct (blake3 always produces 32 bytes), the
unwrap() is unnecessary and obscures the infallibility. Replaced with
explicit `let mut buf = [0u8; 8]; buf.copy_from_slice(...)` which is
equally zero-cost but communicates intent without a panic path.

Affected modules:
- fcp-core/checkpoint.rs: checkpoint_hash test helper
- fcp-core/revocation.rs: BloomFilter::hash_item double-hash extraction
- fcp-mesh/gossip.rs: XorFilterPlaceholder::hash_with_seed

Also adds missing Debug derive to CborTarget enum in fcp-cli/bench/cbor.rs
(required for error messages and test output formatting).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
